name: Ashwagandha
summary: A powerful adaptogenic herb used for centuries in Ayurvedic medicine to help the body manage stress and promote overall vitality.
overview: Ashwagandha is one of the most important herbs in Ayurveda, a traditional system of medicine based on Indian principles of natural healing. It has been used for over 3,000 years to relieve stress, increase energy levels, and improve concentration. The name 'ashwagandha' comes from Sanskrit, meaning 'smell of the horse,' referring both to the herb's unique smell and its ability to increase strength.
howItWorks: Ashwagandha works primarily by modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis, which controls the body's stress response. It helps regulate cortisol levels, the body's primary stress hormone. The active compounds, called withanolides, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It also supports GABA-mimetic activity, which can promote calmness and reduce anxiety.

benefits.name: Stress & Anxiety Reduction
benefits.description: Multiple clinical trials show significant reductions in cortisol levels and perceived stress scores. A 2012 study found a 28% reduction in cortisol levels after 60 days of supplementation.

benefits.name: Sleep Quality
benefits.description: Research indicates improvements in sleep quality and sleep onset latency, particularly with root extract standardized to withanolides.

benefits.name: Physical Performance
benefits.description: Studies suggest improvements in VO2 max, muscle strength, and recovery time in both trained and untrained individuals.

dosages.notes: Look for extracts standardized to 5% withanolides (e.g., KSM-66 or Sensoril)

dosages.timing: Mixed into warm milk or smoothie
dosages.notes: Traditional preparation; lower concentration of active compounds

bestTimeToTake: Morning with breakfast for energy/stress benefits, or evening for sleep support
howLongToWork: Noticeable effects within 2-4 weeks; full benefits at 8-12 weeks of consistent use
whoShouldAvoid: People with autoimmune conditions (may stimulate immune system)
Those with thyroid disorders (consult doctor first)
People scheduled for surgery (stop 2 weeks before)

interactions.description: May increase thyroid hormone levels, potentially requiring medication adjustment
May enhance sedative effects due to GABAergic activity
May counteract immunosuppressive therapy by stimulating immune function

faqs.question: Can I take ashwagandha every day?
faqs.answer: Yes, ashwagandha is generally considered safe for daily use. Most studies use daily supplementation for 8-12 weeks. Some practitioners recommend cycling (e.g., 8 weeks on, 2 weeks off) though this isn't strictly necessary.

faqs.question: Should I take ashwagandha in the morning or at night?
faqs.answer: It depends on your goal. For stress and energy support, morning is ideal. For sleep support, take it 1-2 hours before bed. Some people split their dose between morning and evening.

faqs.question: What's the difference between KSM-66 and Sensoril?
faqs.answer: Both are standardized ashwagandha extracts. KSM-66 is a full-spectrum root extract (more energizing), while Sensoril includes both root and leaf extract (more calming). Both are well-researched and effective.
